description: Read soft signals — tone, mood, energy, sufferings.
purpose: Captures soft, fuzzy signals from the conversation — emotional state, communication style, what the user is feeling but not saying. Reach for this when the Talker needs to react to HOW someone is talking, not WHAT they're saying: a stubborn customer, a confused tone, a frustrated escalation, a hopeful turn. Use Field Extractor instead for hard facts (age, name, intent class, employment). Vibe fields are usually `source: inferred` (read between the lines) but `explicit` is allowed too — same field shape as Field Extractor, just a different mental mode and richer defaults. Defaults to a bigger model and a longer history window because reading vibe needs context. Place upstream of Talker so the response can adapt to the vibe; multiple Vibe Extractors can coexist if you want different dimensions read by different prompts. Phase B: the prompt is one mention-aware string; {{fields_schema}} and {{fields_current}} are auto-injected from the wired fields.

defaultConfig.prompt:
Read the user's vibe — tone, mood, communication style — from patterns across the conversation, not single messages.

JSON keys MUST be the exact field names from the schema — character-for-character. Don't translate, alias, or rephrase them. Values can be in any language; keys must not.

## Vibe to read
{{fields_schema}}

## Currently read
{{fields_current}}

Only include keys you have a confident read for. Omit the rest. Never use null, empty strings, or placeholders. Return {} when nothing was clear enough.
